title: Reconcile every supported statement before approval
description: Account for every source record and prove opening economic balance plus normalized movements equals closing economic balance before a batch becomes committable. Implements G3 and UC-INGEST-001.
rationale: A parser that returns plausible rows without completeness proof cannot populate a trustworthy ledger.
story_want: see proof that the whole statement was interpreted
story_so_that: omitted, duplicated, or sign-inverted rows cannot be approved silently
acceptance_criteria: Given a qualified statement, when preview completes, then every source record has exactly one accepted-candidate, exact-duplicate, excluded-non-transaction, or blocked outcome with a stable reason.
Given any supported initial fixture, when its normalized non-zero movements are summed with its opening economic balance, then the result equals its closing economic balance exactly in minor units.
Given a supported variant that supplies running balances, when records are normalized, then every consecutive running-balance transition reconciles exactly to its signed movement.
Given a source control that the detected variant exposes, when preview completes, then the manifest records and verifies that control exactly.

Given an unexplained transaction-bearing record, amount mismatch, running-balance mismatch, opening/closing mismatch, or ambiguous exclusion, when reconciliation runs, then the manifest remains unapproved and uncommittable.
Given a control type that the source does not expose, when preview completes, then the manifest reports that control as unavailable rather than fabricating a value.
security_criteria: Reconciliation output may expose counts, Boolean outcomes, and safe identifiers, but diagnostics must not expose raw rows, descriptions, balances, or amounts.
